notes: Thrill is a cinematic tension instrument for Kontakt designed for stings, risers, atmospheric suspense, and emotional tension in film and TV scoring. It is built around two primary layers — the Performer layer (bowed and performed string and woodwind textures) and the Texture layer (processed ambient and drone-like sounds) — which combine to create a sense of unease, anticipation, or dread. CC 1 (Modulation) is the primary tension control, pushing intensity from ambient and restrained to full cinematic sting level, making it the most important real-time controller during recording sessions. CC 11 (Expression) provides a finer dynamic shaping tool within the chosen intensity level. The Attack and Release QCs shape whether a texture sweeps in gradually or hits immediately, while the Filter/Brightness QC opens up the high-frequency content for added brightness or closes it down for a darker, more ominous character. Thrill works best with slow, sustained notes and CC 1 automation riding the tension throughout a scene.

Volume.description: Master output level of the instrument

Expression.description: Dynamic expression — fine-grained dynamic shaping within the set tension level

Modulation.description: Tension / Intensity — primary tension control; increasing this pushes from ambient to full cinematic sting intensity

Sustain.description: Sustain pedal — may hold evolving textures or sustain the performer layer

Release Time.description: Release / Tail — controls how long the texture continues after note release

Attack Time.description: Attack — whether the texture builds gradually or hits immediately

Brightness.description: Filter / Brightness — high-frequency filter; open for bright, sharp tension; close for dark, ominous character

Reverb Send.description: Reverb wet level — adds space and depth to the tension textures

Quick Control 1.description: Tension / Intensity — overall tension level; primary expressive control on this instrument

Quick Control 2.description: Performer Layer Level — amount of the live-performed string and woodwind tension layer

Quick Control 3.description: Texture Layer Level — amount of the ambient, processed texture layer beneath the performer

Quick Control 4.description: Reverb — reverb amount for spatial placement from dry to deeply immersive

Quick Control 5.description: Attack — onset speed of the tension texture from gradual swell to immediate sting

Quick Control 6.description: Release / Tail — decay length after the note ends; longer for lingering unease

Quick Control 7.description: Filter / Brightness — low-pass filter cutoff controlling overall tonal darkness or brightness

Quick Control 8.description: Modulation Depth — internal modulation intensity applied to the texture for added motion and instability
